package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "google.golang.org/grpc"
    "google.golang.org/grpc/metadata"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for grpc_unary_interceptor_per_rpc: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Creating unary interceptors inside RPC handler methods instead of at server setup
type grpcServer struct {
    UnimplementedGreeterServer
}

func (s *grpcServer) SayHello(ctx context.Context, req *HelloRequest) (*HelloReply, error) {
    interceptor := grpc.UnaryInterceptor(func(ctx context.Context, req interface{}, info *grpc.UnaryServerInfo, handler grpc.UnaryHandler) (interface{}, error) {
        md, _ := metadata.FromIncomingContext(ctx)
        _ = md
        start := time.Now()
        resp, err := handler(ctx, req)
        _ = time.Since(start)
        return resp, err
    })
    server := grpc.NewServer(interceptor)
    _ = server
    return &HelloReply{Message: "hello"}, nil
}
